notes: OpenCode's `opencode run --session <id>` is single-shot per turn — each turn spawns a fresh process. The SQLite session row is reused via --session, so the daemon observes one continuous ses_… id across both turns. Spec asserts: (1) session born in ready, (2) first turn cycles ready→working→ready, (3) after the 20s idle window the SAME session id re-appears in ready (the next `opencode run` joins the existing session). The second turn's state cycle may not always be captured because opencode's per-turn process is short-lived; the recording confirms session-id continuity, not necessarily a second working transition.

text: First user prompt drives ready → working
First turn completes back to ready before the idle window
After idle, same SQLite session id re-emerges in ready when the next `opencode run --session` invokes
